professionalSummary: Born in the Andes mountains of Ecuador and now based in London, I'm a Full Stack Developer with 5+ years at Engine AI, where I've grown from Junior Developer to Squad Lead. I build AI-powered products — currently leading the team behind our Research Intelligence solution, a RAG and agent-based platform that helps clients extract insights from large document collections.

experience.position: Senior Full Stack Developer & Squad Lead
experience.description: Started as a Junior Developer and grew into a Squad Lead role over the past 5 years at this data insights startup. I've worked on building the core platform and on using it to help clients turn their data into something useful. In my current role as Squad Lead, I'm in charge of making sure the team delivers high-quality features on time — and while I miss hands-on coding, I get to help other people do their best work, which is something I value a lot.
achievements: Developed our Dashboard Engine and API

My first job was to extend what we call our Dashboard Engine — a React app that transforms configuration into full-fledged dashboards with a built-in design system. Alongside that, I built the backend that feeds it the configuration and data it needs.

I built high-stakes, high-reward prototypes for potential clients — the kind where you get one shot to impress. We wanted to show rather than tell, and my eye for making things look good came in very handy.
Squad Lead on a core AI solution

Currently leading the development of our Research Intelligence solution — a platform built on OpenAI and Weaviate that covers the full RAG pipeline: ingestion, chunking, embedding, retrieval, and generation. On top of that there's an agent layer with skills that handle more complex retrieval tasks. The hardest part has been evaluation: figuring out how to reliably measure whether the system is producing good answers, and how new features affect that quality. Running this squad has taught me as much about building AI systems as it has about leading people.

experience.description: Started as part of Jobsity's JSchool program, which I accessed through a hackathon and completed with merits. This led to a full-time Front End Developer position. Jobsity connects Latin American developers with clients from the US and while working there I used my Full Stack skills mainly to develop internal tools.
achievements: Built the React component library that we used for internal and external platforms. I made sure they look good, are reusable and tested/showcased them using Storybook.
While also building the library, I used it on the main platform that helped clients manage their remote teams. That meant building not just the UI but also the backend that powered it.

details: Full merit scholarship awarded by the State
Thesis: Bottle image classification system using Convolutional Neural Networks
Equivalent to Masters in Computer Science (European standard)

strengths.title: Technically proficient
strengths.description: I can jump into a complex codebase or problem with limited context and figure it out. I ask the right questions, make informed decisions where I need to, and try not to let uncertainty become a blocker for the people around me.

strengths.title: Good communicator
strengths.description: I meet people where they are. I listen more than I talk, and I genuinely care about creating an environment where people feel safe being honest. That said, I know when the moment calls for clarity and I don't shy away from saying the important thing.

strengths.title: Design mindset
strengths.description: I love the craft of making something that just feels right to use. I work closely with design teams and I take the experience people have seriously — continuous iteration is how I get there. My favourite kind of design is the kind people don't notice.

currentlyBuilding.title: Research Intelligence Platform
currentlyBuilding.description: Leading the squad building our Research Intelligence solution — helping clients navigate and extract insights from large collections of documents. This has been the perfect opportunity to discover new AI-powered workflows that have changed the way we work and deliver features.

personalProjects.title: AI agent workflows
personalProjects.description: I've been building agents to help with most of my daily work — writing code, planning tasks, doing research, and producing better content. A lot of what I've learned has fed back into how I think about agent design at Engine AI.

personalProjects.title: This website
personalProjects.description: My personal website — built with Astro and Tailwind. An ongoing project I use as an excuse to experiment with design and keep my front-end skills sharp.

personalProjects.title: meal-guru
personalProjects.description: A personal app for meal planning — mostly an attempt to spend less time thinking about what to buy at the supermarket.

thinkingAbout.title: Designing for the assistant
thinkingAbout.description: Most people ask 'how do we rebuild our product so an LLM can do the work?' I think that's the wrong question. The right one is: how do we make our existing product usable by an LLM? That usually means exposing a well-documented, well-abstracted interface — something designed for an assistant to reason about, not just for code to call.
